title: Culture of Human Pluripotent Stem Cells in Naïve Conditions
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3gqad","text":"This protocol is a part of our collection of protocols for culturing human embryos and human stem cell-derived blastoids in an endometrial culture system.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c28hf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qfvu","text":"This protocol is in performed in preparation for the generation of blastoids protocol. These steps can be replaced with any other method for generating blastoids, you may wish to use your own preferred method for doing so here instead of following our protocol. Generally you would choose between these steps and thawing human embryos unless you are doing experiments on blastoids and embryos in parallel.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4j378","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8tc7","text":"This protocol should be undertaken once you have assembled the endometrial scaffold and have begun the hormonal stimulation preparing the scaffold for implantation. This is performed in parallel with a portion of the \"Human Embryo or Blastoid Culture in Endometrial Scaffolds\" protocol, it is recommended that you familiarise yourself with this protocol so that you can time the preparation of the blastoids appropriately to line up with when your scaffold will be ready to receive them, note that this takes longer than thawing embryos so you will need to begin this process earlier.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To generate blastoids for our study, we used the human naïve-state pluripotent cell line HNES1 , which was derived in the following publication: Naive Pluripotent Stem Cells Derived Directly from Isolated Cells of the Human Inner Cell Mass.. Stem cell reports.

We cultured HNES1 cells in N2B27 basal medium supplemented with: 1 micromolar (µM) PD0325901 e.g. PD0325901 10 mg STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #72184 2 micromolar (µM) XAV393 e.g. XAV939 10 mg STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #72674 2 micromolar (µM) Gö 6983 e.g. 10 ng/ml human LIF e.g. Human LIF Recombinant Protein, PeproTech® Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #300-05-25UG on irradiated mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) at 37 °C , 7% CO 2 and 5% O 2 . Cells were passaged as single cells every 3-4 days at a split ratio of 1:4 or 1:5 following dissociation with TrypLE™ Select Enzyme (1X), no phenol red Thermo Fisher Catalog #12563029 for 00:10:00 at Room temperature . A 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor ROCKi Y 27632 dihydrochloride Axon Medchem Catalog #1683 (CAS: 129830-38-2) was added to the PXGL medium for 24 hours after passaging.

Prepare N2B27 basal medium N2B27 basal medium (50mL): A B C D 1 Reagent Stock Concentration Volume (uL) Final Concentration 2 DMEM/F-12 1x 25000 1x 3 Neurobasal 1x 25000 1x 4 B-27 50x 1000 1x 5 N2 100x 250 0.5x 6 L-Glutamine 200mM 500 2mM 7 β-mercaptoethanol 50mM 100 0.1mM 8 Penicillin/Streptomycin 10000U/mL 100 20U/mL Reagents: DMEM/F-12 Advanced DMEM/F-12 Thermo Fisher Catalog #12634010 Neurobasal Neurobasal™ Medium Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #21103049 B-27 B27 supplement minus vitamin A Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #12587010 N2 N2 supplement Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #17502048 L-Glutamine L-Glutamine (200mM) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #25030024 β-mercaptoethanol Gibco™ 2-Mercaptoethanol (50 mM) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #31350010 Penicillin / Streptomycin Penicillin-Streptomycin (10,000 U/mL) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15140122
creator: Richard J Acton
